article_meta.title: Federal immigration officers board an elevator with respondents at immigration court in New York on 17 November 2025

reasoning: Mixed performance: correctly flags loaded author word choice and a defensible slant/structure critique, but commits one clear attribution violation by flagging a quoted phrase as author bias and misses a couple of notable author-level biases.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Flagged "powerful national security agencies," which appears inside a quoted statement by Reynolds — a direct violation of the attribution rule.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: One of three items is not genuine author-level bias (quoted language). The other two are defensible.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed clear author-prose instances such as 'rare rebuke' (subjective qualifier) and 'has repeatedly been accused' (vague attribution/omission of source attribution).
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: For the validly identified items, 'Word Choice' for 'spied' and 'Slant' for structural emphasis are appropriate labels.
